name: Rio Zafrilla and Cuerno de la Cumbre via the Fuente del Tejo trail (PR-CU 16)
name_original: Río Zafrilla y Cuerno de la Cumbre por el sendero Fuente del Tejo (PR-CU 16)
region_intro: Hiking around Zafrilla offers diverse outdoor experiences within the Serrania de Cuenca, a mountainous region characterized by river systems, waterfalls, and unique geological formations. The landscape features the source of the Tagus River and the Ojos del Cabriel, providing tranquil walks along watercourses. The area also includes notable waterfalls such as Cascada del Molino de San Pedro, alongside varied terrain suitable for different hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Zafrilla The most popular hiking route is Ojos del Cabriel and Molino de San Pedro Waterfall Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route explores significant river features and a prominent waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rio Zafrilla and Cuerno de la Cumbre via the Fuente del Tejo trail (PR-CU 16), a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. It offers views of the Zafrilla river and ascends towards Cuerno de la Cumbre. Local hikers also love the Herrería Waterfall – Riverside trail loop from Cascada del Molino de San Pedro, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through riverside environments and past waterfalls, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Zafrilla is defined by its waterfalls, river systems, and the varied mountainous terrain of the Serrania de Cuenca. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate excursions, and more challenging treks. The routes in Zafrilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Zafrilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Zafrilla?
A: Zafrilla offers a selection of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times, providing valuable insights.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Zafrilla?
A: The terrain around Zafrilla is diverse, ranging from tranquil walks along river systems like the Nacimiento del Río Tajo and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;, to more challenging ascents within the mountainous Serrania de Cuenca. You'll find forests, rocky outcrops, and valleys, with trails varying from easy strolls to difficult treks.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Zafrilla?
A: Yes, Zafrilla offers 7 easy trails suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel – Herrería Waterfall loop from El Vallecillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and features a moderate elevation gain.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Zafrilla?
A: Zafrilla is rich in waterfalls. You can explore trails leading to picturesque spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370705369/ojos-del-cabriel-and-molino-de-san-pedro-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and Molino de San Pedro Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that combines river features with a prominent waterfall.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Zafrilla?
A: Beyond the stunning waterfalls, you can discover significant river features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the River Tagus&lt;/a&gt;. There are also viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/603722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Zafrilla?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall – Riverside trail loop from Cascada del Molino de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.2 km loop that takes you through riverside environments and past waterfalls.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Zafrilla?
A: Zafrilla offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time often depends on your preference. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, ideal for enjoying river features, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher trails might be affected by snow.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Zafrilla suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Zafrilla are suitable for families, especially the easier routes that follow river systems or lead to waterfalls. These often have less elevation gain and offer engaging natural features for children to explore. Always check the specific trail details for distance and difficulty to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Zafrilla?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Zafrilla. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected natural areas or when encountering wildlife or other hikers. Please be mindful of local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Zafrilla?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Zafrilla, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning waterfalls, the serene river landscapes, and the diverse mountainous terrain of the Serrania de Cuenca.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Zafrilla?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular starting points for hikes in Zafrilla, especially those leading to major attractions like waterfalls or river sources, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking recommendations for your chosen trail.
tips: It is worth visiting this remote site where tranquility reigns. A beautiful and picturesque landscape that is covered following a gravel road without difficulty that allows us to appreciate the gorges of the Zafrilla river.
